title: balance(): block_height=0 note misclassified as confirmed when scan_tip < min_confirmations
description: Trigger: insert a note with block_height=0 (the migration v4 DEFAULT), then call balance(scan_tip=3, min_confirmations=10). Expected: note is pending (0-0=0 < 10). Actual: threshold = saturating_sub(3,10) = 0; SQL condition 'block_height <= 0' is satisfied by block_height=0 → classified as confirmed. The implementation contradicts the documented formula 'depth = scan_tip - block_height >= min_confirmations' for this edge case. Trigger requires a note at exactly height 0, which currently only arises from the migration DEFAULT or a synthetic test note — not reachable by the live scanner — but the formula divergence is a silent trap for future test authors and any future path that generates height-0 notes.
close_reason: Fixed: balance() uses threshold=-1 when scan_tip < min_confirmations; added regression test balance_height_zero_note_is_pending_when_tip_below_min_confirmations
